{{Infobox ancient site | name = Tangotai Kofun group | native_name = 丹後平古墳群 | native_name_lang = | alternate_name = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | altitude_m =90 | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|40|28|52|N|141|28|1.2|E|display=inline}}; {{Coord|40.481111|N|141.467|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_type = Japan Aomori Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | location =[[:en:Hachinohe, Aomori|Hachinohe, Aomori]], {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}; {{ill|Hachinohe|WD=Q191914}}| region = {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|WD=Q129465|lt=Tōhoku region}} | type = [[kofun]] group | part_of =; {{ill|Excavated Artefacts from Tangotai Kofun Cluster, Aomori Prefecture|WD=Q132706002}}| length = | width = | area = {{convert|7000|ha|acres}} | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 7th to 9th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = 1987 | excavations = 1989-1990 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|40|28|52|N|141|28|1.2|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Tangotai Kofun Cluster'''|丹後平古墳群|Tangotai Kofun-gun}} is an {{ill|Archaeological site|en|Archaeological site|WD=Q839954|lt=Archaeological site}} with a group of circular ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located southwest of the city of [[:en:Hachinohe, Aomori|Hachinohe]] in {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|en|Aomori Prefecture|WD=Q71699|lt=Aomori Prefecture}} in the northern {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|WD=Q129465|lt=Tōhoku region}} of northern {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. In 1999, approximately 7000 hectares of the site received protection as a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] since 1999.<ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web|url=http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/218149|title=丹後平古墳群|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 December 2016}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tangotai site is located on a sloping hill between the {{ill|Mabechi River|en|Mabechi River|WD=Q11668006|lt=Mabechi}} and Dobashi Rivers, near the {{ill|Pacific Ocean|en|Pacific Ocean|WD=Q98|lt=Pacific Ocean}} seacoast. The surrounding area has a high concentration of ruins from the [[Jōmon period|Jōmon]] through [[Kofun period]]s, including the remnants of settlements, graveyards and smaller clusters of ''kofun'' as well as a ceremonial burial site for horses.<ref name= "Bunka1"/>
An estimated 100 tombs were uncovered at the Tangotai site during construction of the “Hachinohe New Town” urban development project in 1987. All were circular {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}-style mound between four and nine meters in diameter, with a central pit containing a wooden coffin, but many of them have a passage-like overhang that is thought to be derived from the entourage of the horizontal hole type stone chamber in front of the pit that holds the casket. In some cases, the casket was resting on a bed of gravel, and in others, it was on charcoal.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tombs are estimated to date from the late 7th century to the mid-9th century, based on the numerous {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} were uncovered from the 29 tombs which were fully [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1987-1988, 1994 to 1998, and 2000. These included shards of {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji}} and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}}, fragments of swords and metal agricultural implements, and thousands of spherical, ''[[magatama]]'' and tubular beads. Of particular interest was a decorated sword hilt discovered in Tomb 15, with a design similar to those made in [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]] on the {{ill|Korea|en|Korea|WD=Q18097|lt=Korea}} in the late 6th century. Within Tomb 25 a ''{{ill|Wadōkaichin|en|Wadōkaichin|WD=Q3565040|lt=Wadōkaichin}}'' coin minted in 708 AD was discovered. In 2018, 195 items were collectively designated a National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property}}<ref name= "Bunka2">{{cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/442713|title=青森県丹後平古墳群出土品|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=5 Jun 2022}}</ref> The relics are on display at the Hachinohe City Museum.
The Tagotai Kofun cluster dates from a time when the area was still populated by the {{ill|Emishi|en|Emishi|WD=Q932574|lt=Emishi}} people and was beyond the control of the central {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|WD=Q909452|lt=imperial government}}, yet it indicates the penetration and survival of late [[Kofun period]] culture beyond the political bounds of the central government, and hints at possible direct contact between the northern {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|WD=Q129465|lt=Tōhoku region}} of Japan and the Korean peninsula in prehistoric times.<ref name= "Bunka2"/>
The site was backfilled after excavation, and is located approximately 10 minutes by car from {{ill|Hachinohe Station|en|Hachinohe Station|WD=Q800866|lt=Hachinohe Station}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Aomori)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Aomori)|WD=Q6573255|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Aomori)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[http://www.city.hachinohe.aomori.jp/index.cfm/12,3241,129,153,html Hachinohe City official home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.pref.aomori.lg.jp/bunka/education/kinen_siseki_11.html Aomori Prefectural government site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.hachinohe.aomori.jp/bunka_sports/bunka/bunkazai/10484.html Hachinohe City government site on excavated items] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.hachinohe.ed.jp/haku/kouko_kofun.html Hachinohe City Museum] {{in lang|ja}}

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Tangotai Kofun group | native_name = 丹後平古墳群 | native_name_lang = | alternate_name = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | altitude_m =90 | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|40|28|52|N|141|28|1.2|E|display=inline}}; {{Coord|40.481111|N|141.467|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_type = Japan Aomori Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | location =[[:en:Hachinohe, Aomori|Hachinohe, Aomori]], {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}; {{ill|Hachinohe|WD=Q191914|1=Hachinohe}}| region = {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|WD=Q129465|lt=Tōhoku region|1=Tōhoku region}} | type = [[kofun]] group | part_of =; {{ill|Excavated Artefacts from Tangotai Kofun Cluster, Aomori Prefecture|WD=Q132706002|1=Excavated Artefacts from Tangotai Kofun Cluster, Aomori Prefecture}}| length = | width = | area = {{convert|7000|ha|acres}} | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 7th to 9th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = 1987 | excavations = 1989-1990 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580|1=Historic Site of Japan}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|40|28|52|N|141|28|1.2|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Tangotai Kofun Cluster'''|丹後平古墳群|Tangotai Kofun-gun}} is an {{ill|Archaeological site|en|Archaeological site|WD=Q839954|lt=Archaeological site|1=Archaeological site}} with a group of circular ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located southwest of the city of [[:en:Hachinohe, Aomori|Hachinohe]] in {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|en|Aomori Prefecture|WD=Q71699|lt=Aomori Prefecture|1=Aomori Prefecture}} in the northern {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|WD=Q129465|lt=Tōhoku region|1=Tōhoku region}} of northern {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. In 1999, approximately 7000 hectares of the site received protection as a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] since 1999.<ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web|url=http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/218149|title=丹後平古墳群|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 December 2016}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tangotai site is located on a sloping hill between the {{ill|Mabechi River|en|Mabechi River|WD=Q11668006|lt=Mabechi|1=Mabechi River}} and Dobashi Rivers, near the {{ill|Pacific Ocean|en|Pacific Ocean|WD=Q98|lt=Pacific Ocean|1=Pacific Ocean}} seacoast. The surrounding area has a high concentration of ruins from the [[Jōmon period|Jōmon]] through [[Kofun period]]s, including the remnants of settlements, graveyards and smaller clusters of ''kofun'' as well as a ceremonial burial site for horses.<ref name= "Bunka1"/>
An estimated 100 tombs were uncovered at the Tangotai site during construction of the “Hachinohe New Town” urban development project in 1987. All were circular {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}-style mound between four and nine meters in diameter, with a central pit containing a wooden coffin, but many of them have a passage-like overhang that is thought to be derived from the entourage of the horizontal hole type stone chamber in front of the pit that holds the casket. In some cases, the casket was resting on a bed of gravel, and in others, it was on charcoal.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tombs are estimated to date from the late 7th century to the mid-9th century, based on the numerous {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} were uncovered from the 29 tombs which were fully [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1987-1988, 1994 to 1998, and 2000. These included shards of {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji|1=Haji pottery}} and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}, fragments of swords and metal agricultural implements, and thousands of spherical, ''[[magatama]]'' and tubular beads. Of particular interest was a decorated sword hilt discovered in Tomb 15, with a design similar to those made in [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]] on the {{ill|Korea|en|Korea|WD=Q18097|lt=Korea|1=Korea}} in the late 6th century. Within Tomb 25 a ''{{ill|Wadōkaichin|en|Wadōkaichin|WD=Q3565040|lt=Wadōkaichin|1=Wadōkaichin}}'' coin minted in 708 AD was discovered. In 2018, 195 items were collectively designated a National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}<ref name= "Bunka2">{{cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/442713|title=青森県丹後平古墳群出土品|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=5 Jun 2022}}</ref> The relics are on display at the Hachinohe City Museum.
The Tagotai Kofun cluster dates from a time when the area was still populated by the {{ill|Emishi|en|Emishi|WD=Q932574|lt=Emishi|1=Emishi}} people and was beyond the control of the central {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|WD=Q909452|lt=imperial government|1=Imperial House of Japan}}, yet it indicates the penetration and survival of late [[Kofun period]] culture beyond the political bounds of the central government, and hints at possible direct contact between the northern {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|WD=Q129465|lt=Tōhoku region|1=Tōhoku region}} of Japan and the Korean peninsula in prehistoric times.<ref name= "Bunka2"/>
The site was backfilled after excavation, and is located approximately 10 minutes by car from {{ill|Hachinohe Station|en|Hachinohe Station|WD=Q800866|lt=Hachinohe Station|1=Hachinohe Station}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Aomori)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Aomori)|WD=Q6573255|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Aomori)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Aomori)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[http://www.city.hachinohe.aomori.jp/index.cfm/12,3241,129,153,html Hachinohe City official home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.pref.aomori.lg.jp/bunka/education/kinen_siseki_11.html Aomori Prefectural government site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.hachinohe.aomori.jp/bunka_sports/bunka/bunkazai/10484.html Hachinohe City government site on excavated items] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.hachinohe.ed.jp/haku/kouko_kofun.html Hachinohe City Museum] {{in lang|ja}}
